behaviors.description: Only files changed in the pull request are analyzed — unchanged files are not scanned.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff (PR changed-file collector -> file-selection filter -> analyzer queue). Unit tests on the filter alone can miss path-normalization or rename-status mismatches between modules. Integration-brake: no required real OS/network/subprocess in behavior text; no AI output dependency; no multi-agent chain. Rejected higher level: behavior text is about selection logic, not real GitHub API/network reliability or runner behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Wire PR-diff parsing + file selection + analysis dispatcher in-process with fixture PR change sets (added/modified/renamed/deleted) and workspace file map; assert only changed file paths are dispatched and unchanged files are never dispatched.

behaviors.description: Findings are grouped by severity (error, warning, info) in the posted PR comment.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is often in wiring between result aggregator and comment renderer/publisher payload shape, not just local grouping logic. Unit would miss contract mismatch (e.g., severity enum mapping breaks template sections). Integration-brake: behavior does not require real HTTP/API call semantics, OS primitives, or model output quality. Rejected System: text requires correct grouping in posted comment content, not transport-layer behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run analyzer-output -> grouping -> markdown comment builder -> publish-request object creation in-process using mixed severities; assert final comment payload has correct severity sections and entries under each section.

behaviors.description: A pull request that touches 200 files completes analysis within the 10-minute Actions timeout.
behaviors.justification: Timeout behavior depends on real runtime characteristics (process startup, filesystem I/O, runner CPU limits, action orchestration overhead). Integration with fakes can pass while real GitHub Actions run exceeds 10 minutes. Lower-level-miss-proof above Integration: an in-process benchmark misses actual CI runner overhead and wall-clock timeout enforcement.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the lint step end-to-end in CI-like environment (same action entrypoint and subprocesses) against a generated/replay PR diff of 200 files; measure wall-clock and assert completion plus comment-post step within 10 minutes.

behaviors.description: When the linter process crashes on a malformed file, the remaining files are still analyzed and results posted.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly involves linter process crash; real subprocess failure modes (signal kill, broken pipes, partial stdout) are OS/process-level. Integration with mocked exceptions can hide crash-handling bugs in process supervision. Rejected Integration via behavior text: 'linter process crashes' requires real subprocess semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Run orchestrator with actual linter subprocess where one crafted malformed file triggers a hard crash/non-zero exit for that invocation; assert supervisor continues processing subsequent files and final PR comment payload is still produced with surviving findings and crash note.

behaviors.description: Duplicate findings on the same line from overlapping rules are deduplicated in the output.
behaviors.justification: Core failure boundary is deterministic dedup logic over finding tuples (file, line, rule/message). No required I/O, subprocess, network, or model dependency. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is pure output dedup semantics, not module orchestration or infrastructure effects.
behaviors.test_description: Test dedup function/object directly with synthetic findings containing same file+line overlaps across rules and unique neighbors; assert only intended duplicates collapse and distinct findings remain.
